prompt:
You and a friend have to meet in Paris tomorrow at noon. Your phone broke so you cannot communicate with him. Where would you go at noon? There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.
responses:
to determine the best meeting spot in paris based on the criteria provided lets evaluate some common landmarks

1 eiffel tower
    uniqueness it is one of the most recognizable structures in paris
    centrality it is not exactly in the center of paris but is a major tourist attraction
    extremeness it is one of the tallest structures in paris

2 arc de triomphe
    uniqueness it is a significant historical monument
    centrality it is located at the western end of the champsélysées
    extremeness it is one of the largest triumphal arches in the world

3 notredame cathedral
    uniqueness it is a famous cathedral with historical significance
    centrality it is located on île de la cité which is considered the heart of paris
    extremeness it is one of the most visited religious buildings in the world

4 louvre museum
    uniqueness it is one of the largest and most famous museums in the world
    centrality it is located in the 1st arrondissement near the center of paris
    extremeness it houses the mona lisa and other worldrenowned artworks

5 place de la concorde
    uniqueness it is a large public square with historical significance
    centrality it is located at the eastern end of the champsélysées and is considered a central point in paris
    extremeness it is one of the largest squares in paris

6 montmartre
    uniqueness it is a hill with a distinct character and the sacrécœur basilica
    centrality it is located in the 18th arrondissement not as central as some other locations
    extremeness it is one of the highest points in paris

now lets apply the criteria

1 uniqueness the eiffel tower stands out as the most unique and recognizable landmark in paris
2 extremeness the eiffel tower is also one of the tallest structures in paris
3 centrality while the eiffel tower is not in the exact center it is a major tourist attraction and easily accessible
4 unique complement none of the other options stand out as uniquely lacking a property

given the criteria the eiffel tower is the best choice because it is unique and extreme and while it is not the most central it is a central point for tourists and a wellknown meeting place

prompt:
You have to split $1000 with another person. You cannot communicate with this person. If you choose the same amount, you win that much money. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose between <answer></answer> tags.
